mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Over any 2-year walk-forward window, the average 20-day forward return for instances with RSAFS above its 12-month moving average is not significantly greater than the overall average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above 0.5% signals the end of the zero-lower-bound period and the start of a tightening cycle, which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.5%, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a broad-based expansion in both production and consumption, reinforcing the economic cycle and supporting equity prices. When one rises and the other falls, the signal weakens.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS are above their respective 6-month moving averages, the 20-day SPX return is not significantly greater than when one or both are below (Wilcoxon p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is above 7%, a decline in UNRATE signals that labor market slack is being absorbed, which is positive for consumer spending and equity markets. In low-unemployment regimes (below 5%), further declines may signal overheating and negative returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 7% and UNRATE declines month-over-month, the 20-day SPX return is not positive on average, or the signal does not reverse sign when UNRATE < 5% (i.e., positive returns in low-unemployment regime).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals an imminent economic contraction, causing investors to reduce risk exposure and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 1% from previous month
failure_condition: Over all instances where INDPRO drops >1% month-over-month, the 20-day SPX return is positive in at least 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy labor market that supports consumer income and spending, thereby driving long-term equity returns upward.
failure_condition: Over 3-year rolling windows, the correlation between PAYEMS 6-month change and SPX 60-day forward return is less than 0.1, or the top-quartile PAYEMS change periods do not produce significantly higher returns than bottom-quartile periods.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) signals that the central bank may be forced to tighten policy aggressively, which is negative for equity valuations. The negative effect is amplified when both conditions are present.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPIAUCSL * (-FEDFUNDS)) does not have a significant negative coefficient in a linear regression predicting 20-day SPX returns (t-stat > -1.96), or the conditional return distribution does not differ from unconditional.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) by more than 5% month-over-month signals a downturn in the housing sector, which is a leading indicator of economic weakness, causing short-term negative equity returns as investors reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% from previous month
failure_condition: On days following a HOUST drop >5%, the 5-day SPX return is negative in less than 60% of cases, or the average return is not significantly below the unconditional average.
